title: 第 80 節
en: Olympos, the abode of Zeus, 657. 'Omne obscurum pro magnifico,' 92. Ongot, supreme spirit of the Tun- guses, 161 n. Ophion, one of the Titans and Gi- gantes, 759. Orchomenos derived fromErekHagim, Ormazd or Ahura Mazda, 126.

— and Ahriman, and Chka'i and Cha'itan, 254. Orsini, had they a bear for their totem? 199. Orthros and Vn'tra, 421-425, 757, — dog of Eurytion, 622, 627. Ortygia, birthplace of Artemis, the returning morn, and Sk. vartika, Osiris, the sun, 162, 167.
— swallowed by Anubis, 167. Osth off quoted, 17, 22.

Otava, the great bear, Finnish god, — sons and daughters of, 266. Oultse-Paz, Mordvinian god of the flocks, 239. Ourania, name of Aphrodite, 731. Ouranos and Varuwa, 216, 387, 390, — and Gaia, their offspring, 533.
Ozai's, Mordvinian spirit or little god, PAASONEN, H., on riddles of the Mordvinians, 92, 100.
Pada, Ahga, and Bha bases, 323, 328. Piida, foot, 592.
— fourth part of anything, 592. Paieon or Paian, identified with Apollon, 702. Paiva, Finnish god of the sun, 265.

— sons and daughters of, 266. Piiivan poika, son of the Sun, or the fire, 266. Paksia-Patiai, Mordvinian goddess of the meadows, 240. Palaiphatos, the Mythographer, 103. Paley, F. A., on Greek mythology explained by the Bible, 214. Palladium, explained as a fetish, 194, Pallas, father of Selene, 89.
— Athene, Dawn, 89. Pan, son of Penelope, 683. Pan-Aryan period, 16, 447. dialects in the, 356.
Pancake tossing in India, and in England, 228. Pandurang, edition of Gaudavaho, 607 n. Pani, 57.
Panope, 'all-seeing,' a wife of Hera- kles, 772. Panu, fire in its divine character, in Finnish, 266. Parallel development of Indian and Greek gods, 499. Paravrigr, 593.
— no name corresponding to, 594. Pargranya, the rain-cloud, 46, 57, 605.

— from a root meaning to sprinkle, — not restricted to one phenomenon, Parigrman, and Amphitryon, 614. Parvati, her worship in Vakpati's time, 606. Patahga, bird, 87. Pater matutinus, 46. Patroklos, 64. Pedu and the horse Paidva, given by the Asvins, 597. Peijot, Finnish name for ancestral spirits, 275. Peirithoos, son of Dia, 523. Pekche-ozais, Mordvinian god of the lime-trees, 244. Pelasgos derived from Peleg, 216. Peleus and Thetis, wedding feast of, and the apple of Eris, 97. Pelias and Neleus, children of Poseidon and Tyro, 578, C48. Pellervo, Finnish guardian spirit of the ploughed field, 271. Penelope, the beloved of Hermes, mother of Pan, 682. Perficus, name for Kronos, 510. Perkun, Lettish god, 434. Perkuna, supreme deity of the Lets, has his wedding in the West, Perse, Perseis, Persephone, and Pn'tha, 617. Persephone, 46.
